acme_cert_fail: Domain %s failed to apply for certificate! Please reinstall using self-signed certificate.
acme_incomplete_state: Current installation is in an incomplete state; fix the issue and reinstall, or run uninstall to clean up.
acme_summary: Resolution correct, using hysteria built-in ACME to apply for certificate. Domain:
auth_secret_prompt: (8/13) Please enter authentication password (default random UUID, strong password recommended):
bandwidth_expectation: Expected speed, the target bandwidth used by the client in Brutal mode. The script will automatically multiply by 1.10 for redundancy; do not set bandwidth higher than the real link limit or it may become unstable.
bbr_profile_aggressive: 3. Aggressive: more aggressive bandwidth grabbing, suitable for good link quality and higher throughput, but more sensitive to fluctuations.
bbr_profile_conservative: 1. Conservative: more conservative and stable convergence, suitable for fluctuating links and average network quality.
bbr_profile_standard: 2. Standard (default): official default preset, balance between speed and stability, suitable for most VPS and home broadband.
bbr_profile_title: BBR provides three adjustment levels for different network environments:
block_http3_disabled: You chose not to block HTTP/3 traffic; QUIC sites will not have hy2 enhancement.
block_http3_enabled: You chose to block HTTP/3 traffic. Depending on your client, block QUIC/HTTP3 traffic or QUIC sites will fail.
block_http3_hint1: If enabled, hysteria2 will not proxy udp/443, QUIC connections will not work, and the client must disable QUIC or connections will fail.
block_http3_hint2: You can also block QUIC/HTTP3/UDP 443 on the client only; the effect is the same.
block_http3_prompt: (12/13) Block HTTP/3 traffic on the server (Hysteria has no congestion-control enhancement for UDP traffic, causing poor performance on QUIC sites like YouTube)?
cert_choice_acme: 1. Apply via ACME (requires tcp/80 port open)
cert_choice_selfsigned: 3. Self-signed certificate (default, recommended: verified via pinSHA256 fingerprint, safe and no domain/80 port needed)
cert_fingerprint_fail: Failed to calculate certificate fingerprint, falling back to insecure connection (insecure=1) mode.
cert_move_ca: Moving CA certificate to result directory...
cert_pinsha256_hint: The client will verify this fingerprint via pinSHA256; insecure connection is disabled by default.
cert_server_key_csr: Generating server private key and CSR...
client_only_block_http3_tip: Tip: It is recommended to enable blocking QUIC/HTTP3/UDP 443 on the client for a better experience.
congestion_bbr_hint: BBR: more aggressive, usually higher throughput, suitable for speed-oriented scenarios.
congestion_brutal_hint: Brutal: Hysteria 2 exclusive feature, fixed-rate model. More worth trying in harsh network environments, especially when real link bandwidth is known and stronger anti-jitter/bandwidth-grabbing capability is desired.
congestion_reno_hint: Reno: more conservative and stable, suitable for scenarios prioritizing compatibility and stability.
congestion_selected_bbr: Selected congestion control mode: BBR / BBR preset:
delay_prompt: (5/13) Please enter average latency to this server, used by Brutal mode to estimate QUIC window (default 200, unit: ms):
dns_acme_summary: You selected ACME DNS validation to apply for certificate:
dns_resolution_failed: Domain resolution failed, no DNS records (A/AAAA) found. Please check the domain resolves to this machine!
domain_ip_mismatch: The IP resolved by the domain does not match this machine's IP, please re-enter!
domain_requirement: (must resolve to this machine, CDN off)
download_prompt: (6/13) Please enter desired client download speed (default 50, unit: mbps):
enter_local_ip: Please enter the local IP yourself:
error_root_required: Please run this script with root privileges!
fixed_hop_interval_error: Fixed hop interval format error; please enter seconds not less than 5s, e.g. 30s
fixed_hop_interval_prompt: Please enter fixed hop interval (default 30s, cannot be lower than 5s):
hihy_cmd_install_fail: hihy command installation failed, please check network or write permissions and retry.
install_success: Installation successful, please see the detailed configuration below
ip_prompt: Please enter the correct public IP (do not add [] for IPv6 addresses):

local_cert_summary: You selected local certificate encryption. Domain:
local_ip_fetch_failed: Failed to get local IP, please check network connection! curl -s -m 8 ip.sb
local_key_path_prompt: Please enter certificate key file path:
masquerade_choice_file: 3. file (acts as a static file server from a directory; directory must contain index.html)
masquerade_choice_proxy: 2. proxy (acts as a reverse proxy and serves content from another website)
masquerade_file_prompt: Please enter masquerade website file directory (default: /etc/hihy/file; mikutap will be auto-downloaded):
masquerade_proxy_hint: Reverse-proxy this site but do not replace domain names inside the page.
masquerade_proxy_prompt: Please enter masquerade proxy address (default: https://www.helloworld.org):
masquerade_stuff_prompt: Please enter HTTP masquerade header content-stuff (default: HelloWorld):
masquerade_tcp_disabled: You chose not to listen on tcp/%s port
masquerade_tcp_enabled: You chose to also listen on tcp/%s port
masquerade_tcp_hint1: Usually websites only offer HTTP/3 as an upgrade option.
masquerade_tcp_hint2: Listening on a TCP port to serve masquerade content makes the masquerade more natural; if disabled, browsers without H3 will not reach the masquerade content.
masquerade_tcp_prompt: (11/13) Also listen on tcp/%s to enhance masquerade behavior?
max_hop_interval_error: Maximum hop interval format error; please enter seconds >= minimum interval, e.g. 30s
max_hop_interval_prompt: Please enter maximum hop interval (default 30s, must be >= minimum interval):
menu_hint_hihy_cmd: Tip: run %s command to execute this script again.
menu_wait_continue: Press any key to return to main menu...
min_hop_interval_error: Minimum hop interval format error; please enter seconds not less than 5s, e.g. 10s
min_hop_interval_prompt: Please enter minimum hop interval (default 10s, cannot be lower than 5s):
non_brutal_skip: (5-7/13) Non-Brutal mode selected, skipping latency and upload/download input, using local congestion controller config.
notify_core_update: [!] hysteria2 core update available, version: %s   log: https://v2.hysteria.network/docs/Changelog/
notify_hihy_update: [☺] hihy update available, version: %s, it is recommended to update and check the logs: https://github.com/emptysuns/Hi_Hysteria/
obfs_choice_gecko: 3. gecko (experimental) - additionally splits QUIC handshake packets on top of salamander, stronger anti-detection
obfs_choice_salamander: 2. salamander - obfuscates packets into random featureless bytes
obfs_enabled: You will use %s obfuscation to encrypt traffic
obfs_hint: Tip: Using obfs obfuscation makes traffic harder to identify as UDP. However, it increases CPU load and may reduce peak speed. If you pursue performance and are not specifically targeted, it is recommended not to use it.
path_not_exist: Path does not exist, please re-enter!
port_bind_fail: Port is in use, please change port!
port_hint: There is no evidence that non-udp/443 ports will be blocked; it is only a possible better camouflage measure. If you use port hopping, a random port is recommended here.
port_hopping_disabled: You chose not to use port hopping.
port_hopping_enabled: You chose to enable Port Hopping / multi-port.
port_hopping_intro: Tip: Long-term single-port UDP connections are easily blocked/QoS/throttled by ISPs. Enabling this can effectively avoid the problem.
port_hopping_mode_prompt: Please select port hopping interval mode:
port_hopping_range_hint: Port Hopping needs multiple ports. Make sure these ports are not used by other services. Hysteria 2 native range listening will be used; the server will automatically handle forwarding in the range.
port_in_use: Port %s is in use, PID: %s! Please re-enter or run kill -9 %s and reinstall.
port_prompt: (2/11) Please enter the port you want to open. This is the server port, 443 is recommended (default random 10000-65535)
prompt_enter_number_or_default: Enter number (press Enter to use default):
public_ip_check: Is the address used by clients to connect correct? Public IP:
realm_address_empty: Address cannot be empty, please re-enter:
realm_address_prompt: Please enter rendezvous server address (format: host:port or host):
realm_intro_line1: Realm is Hysteria 2's P2P NAT traversal mode. It introduces both sides via a rendezvous server for UDP hole punching.
realm_intro_line2: After punching succeeds, traffic goes directly peer-to-peer without passing through the rendezvous server.
realm_intro_line3: The server can run without a public IP or port forwarding.
realm_intro_line4: Use cases: NAT / home broadband / CGNAT / environments without a public IP. Details: https://hysteria.network/zh/docs/advanced/Realms/
realm_name_label: Your Realm name (do not leak; anyone who knows this name can get your server's IP):
realm_password_prompt: Please enter rendezvous server password (default: public):
realm_prompt_title: (0/13) Use Realm mode (P2P NAT traversal, no public IP needed):
realm_server_official_hint: The official server address is realm.hy2.io, use the default password public, no change needed.
realm_skip_masquerade_tcp: Realm mode does not need TCP listening, skipping masquerade port settings
realm_skip_port: Realm mode does not need port configuration, skipping port settings
realm_skip_port_hopping: Realm mode does not need port hopping, skipping this setting
realm_warning_core_only: Warning: currently only supported when running with hysteria core directly.
remarks_prompt: (13/13) Please enter client name remark (default uses domain or IP; e.g. enter test and the name will be Hy2-test):
self_assign_ip_prompt: Multiple IPs or DNS may not be active. If you are sure it resolves to this machine, do you want to specify the local IP yourself? [y/N]:
selfsigned_cert_summary_ip: You selected self-signed %s certificate encryption. Public IP:
selfsigned_cert_summary_realm: You selected self-signed %s certificate encryption. Rendezvous URI:
selfsigned_domain_prompt: Please enter the domain for the self-signed certificate (default: helloworld.com):
start_port_greater_error: Start port must be less than end port, please re-enter!
this_option_cannot_be_empty: This option cannot be empty, please re-enter!
unknown_error: Unknown error: please check the error below and submit an issue to GitHub
unknown_error_incomplete_state: Incomplete installation state has been kept; fix the issue and reinstall, or run uninstall to clean up.
upload_prompt: (7/13) Please enter desired client upload speed (default 10, unit: mbps):
warp_install_done: WARP installed. Hysteria 2 will connect via Cloudflare WARP IP.
warp_install_fail_exit: WARP was not installed successfully, terminating script execution
warp_install_prompt: (Optional) Install global WARP [fscarmen] on the server and use Cloudflare WARP IP for hole punching?
warp_installed_hint: WARP is already installed. Use the warp d command to obtain dual-stack WARP IPs.
warp_manual_cmd: Please run manually: wget -N https://gitlab.com/fscarmen/warp/-/raw/main/menu.sh && bash menu.sh d
warp_principle_line1: Principle: WARP connects to Cloudflare's global edge network via the WireGuard protocol and assigns a WARP IP to the server.
warp_principle_line2: Hysteria 2 uses this WARP IP for Realm hole punching; the client actually connects to a Cloudflare edge node.
warp_principle_line3: This hides the server's real IP, effectively running Hysteria 2 behind Cloudflare CDN.
warp_principle_line4: Note: Because Cloudflare WARP is NAT4, the client must have a public IP or be NAT1/2 (usually fine).
warp_reenabling: Re-enabling WARP to ensure a stable connection...
warp_select_global_mode: In the WARP install menu, select [Global] mode (choose global when the menu appears).
warp_skip: Skipping WARP installation, using the server's real IP.
client_config_masquerade_tcp_not_listene: ⚠️  Note: masquerade is not listening on a TCP port.
client_config_masquerade_h3_hint: 💡 You may need to %s in your browser to access it.
client_config_pinsha256_safe_hint: 🔒 You are using a self-signed certificate. The client has verified the certificate fingerprint via %s, so the connection is secure and insecure mode is not needed.
client_config_masquerade_trust_hint: To visit the masquerade site in a browser, you can trust the certificate manually or set hosts to point the domain.
client_config_selfsigned_verify_hint: 🔒 You are using a self-signed certificate. To verify the masquerade site:
client_config_selfsigned_verify_step1: 1. Trust the certificate in your browser manually.
client_config_selfsigned_verify_step2: 2. Set hosts so the IP points to the domain.
client_config_realm_mode_desc: 🌐 Realm mode - The server connects via P2P hole punching; no public IP or port needed.
client_config_realm_client_support_warni: ⚠ Please make sure your client supports Hysteria2 Realm mode.
client_config_realm_server_password: Use the rendezvous address above as the server field in the client config; authentication password: %s
client_config_realm_share_link_title: 🔗 2. [hysteria2+realm sharing link] For clients that support Realm URI:
client_config_share_link_title: 🔗 2. [v2rayN-Windows / v2rayN-Android / nekobox / passwall / Shadowrocket] sharing link:
client_config_realm_no_clashmeta_hint: Hint: Realm mode does not yet support ClashMeta config; please use the sharing link above or the native config file.
client_config_native_file_realm: 📄 2. [Recommended] [Nekoray / V2rayN / NekoBox for Android] native config file, fastest updates, full parameters, best effect. File path: %s
client_config_native_file_standard: 📄 3. [Recommended] [Nekoray / V2rayN / NekoBox for Android] native config file, fastest updates, full parameters, best effect. File path: %s
